title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Kennedy, Jiles, and Gwendolyn initially assume that Lily Kimball has simply missed her train or changed her mind regarding her visit. However, after Dr. Kennedy calls the train station and finds no record of her arrival, Officer Rast of the Longford Police Department calls to report that Lily has been found dead.

Officer Rast informs them that Lily was strangled and her body was discovered by a hiker in a wooded area near Midgehams Junction. Although Dr. Kennedy had sent Lily a letter with specific directions to his home, Rast reveals that Lily arrived much earlier than expected; she departed Cumbria at 1:30 PM and alighted at Midgehams Junction, the station preceding Woodruff, rather than following the doctor's instructions.

During an inquiry at the police department, Kennedy, Jiles, and Gwendolyn recount the history of Mrs. Harryd, who was Dr. Kennedy’s sister and the stepmother of Jiles and Gwendolyn. Eighteen years prior, Mrs. Harryd disappeared from the family home, an event previously attributed to elopement. Lily Kimball, formerly known as Lily Abbot, had served as the household's housekeeper during that time. Lily had long suspected a murder had occurred in the house, a suspicion that persisted even after Colonel Harryd died in a mental institution under the delusion that he had killed his wife.

Officer Rast suggests Lily may have been involved in extortion or was attempting to meet someone else. Lily's husband, Mr. Kimball, testifies that Lily was obsessed with crime stories and frequently claimed a husband had killed his wife and hidden the body, though he dismissed her theories as mere imagination. Following the investigation, a terrified Gwendolyn returns home to be cared for by Jiles under Dr. Kennedy's instructions.
